teachingPoint: Tenant bootstrap is all-or-nothing: record + FREEDOM config + status update + audit must all succeed before TenantProvisioned emits. A partial bootstrap (record exists, config missing) creates a silently broken tenant where every downstream flow falls back to wrong defaults.
positiveExample: SETNX(hash(operatorId+tenantSlug)) at ORDER 1; storeDocument(tenant, status:PROVISIONING) at ORDER 2; bulkSeedFreedomConfig(tenantId, tier) synchronous blocking at ORDER 3; updateDocument(status:ACTIVE) at ORDER 4; storeDocument(audit) at ORDER 5; enqueue(TenantProvisioned) at ORDER 6.
negativeExample: Fire-and-forget config seeding: storeDocument(tenant) then enqueue(TenantProvisioned) then configSeed.seed().catch(() => {}). Tenant exists, event fires, downstream flows start — but config seed fails silently. Every FREEDOM config lookup returns null, falls back to platform defaults that don't match the tier.
discriminatingConstraint: bulkSeedFreedomConfig MUST be synchronous and blocking — not async, not fire-and-forget. TenantProvisioned is emitted ONLY after all 4 steps confirm. TenantProvisioningFailed with stepFailed on any step 2-4 failure.

teachingPoint: Machine-locked keys (tenantId, masterTenantId, provisionedAt, subscriptionTier) are compile-time constants — never stored in a database or FREEDOM config. Key mutability is validated at ORDER 1 before any storage access. A runtime lookup for locked keys is attackable: write to the locked-key list first, then override tenantId.
positiveExample: const MACHINE_LOCKED_KEYS = ['tenantId','masterTenantId','provisionedAt','subscriptionTier']; if (MACHINE_LOCKED_KEYS.includes(key)) { emit ConfigKeyImmutable; return; } — ORDER 1, no OCC read, no value validation, no storage for locked keys.
negativeExample: const locked = await db.getDocument('locked-keys-config'); if (locked.keys.includes(key)) reject; — Runtime lookup means attacker writes 'locked-keys-config' to remove tenantId from list, then overrides tenantId via config update. Silent cross-tenant corruption.
discriminatingConstraint: MACHINE_LOCKED_KEYS is a compile-time constant array. ORDER 1 rejection with no storage access for locked keys. ConfigKeyImmutable emitted immediately. storeDocumentWithOCC for mutable keys — not plain storeDocument.

teachingPoint: Quota counters MUST be written in a single Redis MULTI/EXEC atomic block. Individual SET commands in a loop leave inconsistent per-API-type quotas on crash — user hits new limit on API calls but old limit on storage because crash happened between SET #5 and SET #6.
positiveExample: const tierDef = await tierService.getQuotas(subscriptionTier); const result = await quotaRedis.multiExec(tenantId, [['SET', key1, val1], ['SET', key2, val2], ...]); — all or none.
negativeExample: for (const [type, limit] of Object.entries(quotas)) { await redis.set(`quota:${tenantId}:${type}`, limit); } — crash at iteration 5 leaves 5 counters at new values and 3 at old values. Inconsistent enforcement.
discriminatingConstraint: Redis MULTI/EXEC atomic block for ALL quota counters — no individual SET loop. Quota values from tier definitions at runtime — never hardcoded literals. TenantConfigurationUpdated processed ONLY if key.startsWith('quota_').

teachingPoint: Tenant suspension is a status change ONLY — never deleteDocument. Deletion on suspension is irreversible data loss; reactivation becomes impossible. cascadeToSubscriptions:true MUST be present on TenantSuspended — without it, FLOW-12 billing continues for a suspended tenant.
positiveExample: await db.updateDocument('xiigen-tenants', tenantId, {status:'SUSPENDED', accessBlockedAt}); await audit.storeDocument({...}); await queue.enqueue('TenantSuspended', {tenantId, cascadeToSubscriptions:true});
negativeExample: await db.deleteMany('xiigen-subscriptions', {tenantId}); await queue.enqueue('TenantSuspended', {tenantId}); — Irreversible deletion of subscriptions. No cascadeToSubscriptions field means FLOW-12 T210 never processes bulk suspension — billing continues.
discriminatingConstraint: updateDocument(status:SUSPENDED) ONLY on suspension — no deleteDocument anywhere. TenantSuspended.cascadeToSubscriptions = true always (machine field). Termination delegates purge to FLOW-13 T216 via DataPurgeRequested — never inline deleteAll.

teachingPoint: tenantId is ALWAYS computed internally (hash of tenantSlug + registration timestamp) or read from AsyncLocalStorage — NEVER from request payload. Request body tenantId is ignored on all 4 services. Violating this allows an operator to claim another tenant's ID.
positiveExample: T605: tenantId = hash(tenantSlug + registrationTimestamp) — computed internally. T606/T607/T608: tenantId from ALS (TenantContextMiddleware) — request body tenantId field ignored even if present.
negativeExample: const tenantId = event.tenantId; await db.storeDocument('xiigen-tenants', {tenantId, ...}); — Operator A sends tenantId of Operator B. All records stored under B's ID. Cross-tenant data corruption.
discriminatingConstraint: T605: tenantId = internal hash (not from event). T606/T607/T608: tenantId exclusively from ALS. Request body tenantId rejected on all paths. billingContactEmail absent from xiigen-tenants storeDocument (PII in tenant index).

teachingPoint: Termination delegates purge via DataPurgeRequested to FLOW-13 T216 — never inline deleteAll. tombstoneRef = hash(tenantId + 'terminate' + timestamp) — one-way hash with no raw PII. TenantTerminated payload carries tombstoneRef, cancelledAt, dataRetentionDays — no raw tenant data.
positiveExample: await queue.enqueue('TenantTerminated', {tenantId, tombstoneRef, cancelledAt, dataRetentionDays}); await queue.enqueue('DataPurgeRequested', {tenantId, tombstoneRef, purgeScope:'ALL_TENANT_DATA'});
negativeExample: await db.deleteAll('xiigen-*', {tenantId}); — Inline deletion bypasses purge audit trail, makes recovery impossible, violates data retention compliance. No tombstoneRef for FLOW-19 saga compensation.
discriminatingConstraint: DataPurgeRequested emitted in same handler as TenantTerminated. tombstoneRef is a one-way hash — no raw data. dataRetentionDays from FREEDOM config — not hardcoded. Never inline deleteAll.

teachingPoint: All-or-nothing multi-resource provisioning. SETNX idempotency at ORDER 1, then synchronous blocking write sequence across multiple storage systems. Partial provision is silent corruption.
ironRules: SETNX(hash(operatorId+tenantSlug)) at ORDER 1 before any storeDocument
bulkSeedFreedomConfig synchronous and blocking at ORDER 3
TenantProvisioned emitted ONLY after all 4 steps confirm
TenantProvisioningFailed with stepFailed on any step 2-4 failure
codeSnippet: if (!await redis.setnx(key)) return idempotent; await db.storeDocument(tenant, {status:'PROVISIONING'}); await configSeed.bulkSeed(tenantId, tier); await db.updateDocument(tenantId, {status:'ACTIVE'}); await db.storeDocument(audit); await queue.enqueue('TenantProvisioned', payload);

teachingPoint: Compile-time constant array of immutable keys. ORDER 1 rejection with zero storage access. Runtime key lookup is attackable — write to locked-key list first, then override protected key.
ironRules: MACHINE_LOCKED_KEYS is compile-time constant — never database or FREEDOM config
ORDER 1 check before any OCC read or value validation
ConfigKeyImmutable emitted immediately on locked key rejection
storeDocumentWithOCC for mutable keys — not plain storeDocument
codeSnippet: const MACHINE_LOCKED_KEYS = ['tenantId','masterTenantId','provisionedAt','subscriptionTier']; if (MACHINE_LOCKED_KEYS.includes(key)) { await queue.enqueue('ConfigKeyImmutable', {tenantId, key, reason:'MACHINE_LOCKED'}); return; }

teachingPoint: Redis MULTI/EXEC atomic block for all quota counters. Individual SET in a loop leaves inconsistent per-API-type quotas on crash. Tier definitions loaded at runtime — never hardcoded.
ironRules: All quota writes in single MULTI/EXEC block — never individual SET loop
Quota values from ITenantTierService — never hardcoded literals
codeSnippet: if (!key.startsWith('quota_')) return; const tierDef = await tierService.getQuotas(tier); const result = await quotaRedis.multiExec(tenantId, tierDef.entries.map(e => ['SET', `quota:${tenantId}:${e.type}`, String(e.limit)]));

teachingPoint: Suspension = status change ONLY. Never deleteDocument on any suspension path. cascadeToSubscriptions:true always present. Termination delegates purge via DataPurgeRequested — never inline deleteAll.
ironRules: TenantTerminated + DataPurgeRequested BOTH emitted — never inline deleteAll
tombstoneRef = hash(tenantId + 'terminate' + timestamp) — no raw PII
codeSnippet: await db.updateDocument('xiigen-tenants', tenantId, {status:'SUSPENDED'}); await audit.storeDocument({...}); await queue.enqueue('TenantSuspended', {tenantId, suspendedAt, reason, cascadeToSubscriptions: true});
